name: MacQ-mCitrine
description: Early opsin-fluorescent protein FRET voltage indicator, fusing the Mac rhodopsin to mCitrine.

spectrum.name: MacQ-mCitrine
spectrum.note: Verbatim FPbase mCitrine spectrum (the FRET-donor fluorophore in MacQ-mCitrine; correlation 1.00, max difference 0.00 vs FPbase mCitrine ex/em). MacQ is an eFRET sensor — the mCitrine donor's emission is quenched by FRET to the voltage-dependent Mac rhodopsin acceptor, so the displayed curve is the mCitrine fluorophore. Both arrays normalized to max = 1.0.

voltage.name: MacQ-mCitrine
voltage.note: Re-digitized the green MacQ-mCitrine ΔF/F–voltage curve from Fig. 3b (color-separated from the near-coincident orange MacQ-mOrange2 trace; legend line masked), calibrated to the ±10% y-ticks and −100→+50 mV x-axis, rebaselined so −70 mV = 0 (the raw curve already crosses ~0 near −70 mV). Monotonic negative-going; saturates near −13% above 0 mV. The −70→+30 mV chord is ~−13% (vs the stored dynamicRange −20%, which is the paper's local linear slope around −70 mV). Highest step is +34 mV (paper's last command voltage), not +40. Previous stored curve ([8,3,0,-2,-5,-8,-10,-12,-14]) used a mis-scaled y-axis (box edges assumed as ±10/−20 ticks) and was too shallow at the depolarized end.

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted means of the bi-exponential fits in Supplementary Table 1 (n=3 cells). Activation (depolarizing, +): τfast 2.8 ms (74%), τslow 71 ms → τon = 0.74×2.8 + 0.26×71 = 20.5 ms. Deactivation (hyperpolarizing, −): τfast 5.4 ms (77%), τslow 67 ms → τoff = 0.77×5.4 + 0.23×67 = 19.6 ms. Previously stored the fast components alone (2.8 / 5.4 ms) — the paper's headline ~5 ms (1−1/e) rise is fast-component-dominated, but the ~23–26% slow tail (~70 ms) dominates the amplitude-weighted mean. Temperature not stated; room temperature (~22°C) assumed for the in vitro perfusion-chamber recordings.

dynamicRangeData.note: Steady-state ~20% ΔF/F per 100 mV, computed from a linear fit of the ΔF/F–voltage relationship around the −70 mV holding potential (vs ~35% for ArcLight-239Q). This is the local slope at −70 mV; the F-V curve (Fig. 3b) saturates above 0 mV, so the −70→+30 mV chord reads only ~−12%. 1P widefield, 505 nm, cultured neurons.
19.49 ± 2.03% ΔF/F per 100 mV in C. elegans pharyngeal muscle, calibrated against simultaneous sharp-electrode voltage recordings (Azimi-Hashemi et al. 2019). 1P widefield (189 fps).

sensitivityData.note: Peak ΔF/F to a single action potential in cultured neurons (4.8 ± 0.2%, mean ± s.e.m., n=12 cells); MacQ-mOrange2 was 7.2 ± 0.4%. 1P widefield, 505 nm.

apWidthData.sample: Cultured rat hippocampal neurons, in vitro
apWidthData.note: Derived by digitizing the green MacQ-mCitrine averaged optical single-AP waveform (mean of n=16 spikes) in Fig. 5c: calibrated time from the 20 ms scale bar, traced the ΔF/F curve, and measured the full-width at half-maximum (baseline from the flat pre-spike segment; peak amplitude ≈4% ΔF/F, consistent with the 4.8% peak-per-AP value). FWHM ≈ 10.2 ms — much broader than the simultaneously recorded electrical AP (3.2 ± 0.6 ms FWHM, main text) because MacQ-mCitrine's ~20 ms off-kinetics broaden the optical response. Kinetics-dominated (FWHM ≫ the 2.27 ms frame period at 440 Hz), so the frame rate does not materially set the width. 1P widefield, 15 mW/mm², 505 nm.

photostabilityData.note: Derived from the photobleaching rate of 1.3 ± 0.1 %/s (Supplementary Table 1, n=10 cells, 15 mW/mm², 505 nm): 100 − 1.3×30 s ≈ 61% remaining after 0.5 min.

brightnessData.note: The paper reports a two- to four-fold brightness increase over ArcLight; 3× is the representative midpoint. (Fig. S4 plots SNR / photon-detection rate, not a direct brightness ratio.)
